title: Overtime Pay Tax Relief Act of 2025
short_title: Overtime Pay Tax Relief Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill gives workers a tax break on overtime pay they earn at their jobs. Workers can deduct some of their overtime pay from their taxes, but only up to 20% of their regular wages from the same employer. The tax break is only available to people who make less than $100,000 per year, or $150,000 for single parents, or $200,000 for married couples filing together. This tax deduction will only last until the end of 2029.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Overtime Pay Tax Relief Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Deduction for Overtime Compensation
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to let workers deduct some of their overtime pay from their taxes. Workers can deduct overtime pay up to twenty percent of their regular wages from the same employer in a tax year. Overtime compensation means the extra pay required by section seven of the Fair Labor Standards Act of nineteen thirty eight. This law sets minimum wage and overtime rules for most workers. However, this tax break is only available to people who earn less than certain amounts each year. Married couples filing taxes together must earn less than two hundred thousand dollars. People who are head of household must earn less than one hundred fifty thousand dollars. All other individuals must earn less than one hundred thousand dollars. This tax deduction will end after December thirty first two thousand twenty nine. The section also makes technical changes to the tax code so this deduction works properly. It allows people to take this deduction even if they do not itemize other deductions on their tax return. The Treasury Department must update tax withholding tables to account for this new deduction. These changes apply to overtime pay received after this law is signed.
